zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside是Margaret Girling的兒子，於1500年7月26日出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾賀特福德。他於1520年與Emma Elizabeth Grey在英國英格蘭賀特福爾賀特福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1572年10月12日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾維斯特米爾享年72歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特霍爾斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside是Margaret Girling的儿子，于1500年7月26日出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾賀特福德。他于1520年与Emma Elizabeth Grey在英国英格兰賀特福爾賀特福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1572年10月12日在英国英格兰賀特福爾維斯特米爾享年72岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特霍爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Silverside
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English silver ‘silver’ + side ‘side (of the body, ?head)’. Compare Richard Silvereghe (‘silver eye’), 1414–15 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Yorks); Adam Siluermouth (‘silver mouth’), 1379 in Yorks Poll Tax ; John Silvertop (‘silver hair’), 1478 in York Freemen’s Register . Some early bearers may belong with sense 2.
2  English: locative name from Silver Side in Farlam (Cumb), so recorded in 1485.

variants.name: Yorks Poll Tax
meanings: 1 English: relationship name, ‘son of Dawe’. The Middle English personal name Daw(e) is sometimes a pet form of David (See (3) below) but more generally of Rauf or Raw (i.e. Ralph), of which Daw(e) is a rhyming form ( See Daw ). Dawson is one of the most numerous patronymic surnames, and Ralph was a much commoner personal name than David, especially in the north Midlands and north of England, where most names in -son have arisen.

2 English: relationship name, ‘son of Daud(e)’, another Middle English pet form of Ralph ( See Daud ). Daude and Dawe were evidently used interchangeably. John Dauson is listed with Beatrix Daudewyf, 1379 in Yorks Poll Tax (Middleton near Ilkeley, WR Yorks), and is probably her son. Daudeson would probably have merged with Dawson or else Dodson .
3 Scottish: relationship name, ‘son of Dawe’, in Scotland a diminutive of David .

variants.name: York Freemen’s Register
meanings: 1 English: (i) nickname from Middle English kei, ka ‘left-handed’ or ‘clumsy’ (Old Scandinavian kei ‘left-handed’). The word, in the sense ‘left (hand or foot)’, survived in Lancs and Cheshire dialect until at least the 19th century, and must formerly have been widespread in counties further south. Compare Thomas Kafot, 1185 in Templars Records (Lincs), Matilda Cafot’, 1319 in Subsidy Rolls (London), Willelmus Cayfote, 1327 in Subsidy Rolls (Worcs), John Kayfot, 1327 in Subsidy Rolls (Thornbury, Gloucs), and the 1296 Sussex example le Kay cited below. For the spelling key, note the translation of French gauchier as ‘left-handed, key-fisted’ in Cotgrave’s Dictionary of French and English Tongues (1611). (ii) nickname from northern Middle English ka, kae, kay ‘jackdaw’ (Old Scandinavian ká). Reaney, Dictionary notes that in York Freemen’s Register the surname occurs as Kaa (1433) and Caa (1461), and is apparently interchangeable with Cay and Kay. This derivation is restricted to the northern counties and perhaps N Lincs, for in the Midlands and southern English counties ka became co(o) by the 13th century ( see Coe ). It is probably also not a source of Key(es), unless by confusion with (i). (iii) nickname, occasionally perhaps from Middle English kai(e), kei(e) ‘key’, applied to a maker of keys ( compare Kear ), or alluding to the office of keeper or bearer of keys, but clear evidence for this is wanting.
2 Irish, Manx, Scottish: reduced form of Gaelic O’Kea, McKee , or McKay . Some of the following Scottish bearers may alternatively belong with (1).

3 English: locative name from Middle English kay(e), key(e), keay ‘quay’ (Old French kay, cay), for someone who lived near or worked at a wharf or quay. Compare Atkey . The 1600–1 bearer may alternatively belong with (2), and some post-medieval bearers under (1) could belong here.

variants.name: Inquisitiones post Mortem
meanings: Norman: (i) nickname from Anglo-Norman French lovel ‘wol fcub’, a diminutive of lou, love ‘wolf’. Richard Luvel, 1263 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Somerset), was ‘of Kari Lowel barony’, and was descended from William, Earl of Yvery, who was called Lupellus to distinguish him from his father Robert who had acquired the nickname Lupus because of his violent temper. Here the diminutive is clearly used in the sense ‘the younger’. Compare Low . There may have been some confusion with Lovewell . (ii) relationship name; Lovel is also found as an Anglo-Norman French personal name, and is probably another source of the surname. Compare Love , Lovett ,

variants.name: Silversides
meanings: English: variant of Silverside with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside was born on 26 July 1500, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Margaret Girling. He married Emma Elizabeth Grey in 1520, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 12 October 1572, in Westmill, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72, and was buried in Hawkhurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside est né le 26 juillet 1500, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Margaret Girling. Il a épousé Emma Elizabeth Grey en 1520, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 12 octobre 1572, à Westmill, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Hawkhurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside wurde am 26. Juli 1500 in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Margaret Girling geboren. Er hat 1520 in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom Emma Elizabeth Grey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 12. Oktober 1572 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Westmill, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hawkhurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside è nato il il 26 luglio 1500 nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Margaret Girling. Lui sposato Emma Elizabeth Grey nel 1520, nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 ottobre 1572, nel Westmill, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Hawkhurst, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Silversideは1500年7月26日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードでMargaret Girlingの息子として生まれた。1520年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードでEmma Elizabeth Greyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1572年10月12日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアウエストミルで72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントハウクハルストにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside(은)는 1500년 7월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 Margaret Girling의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 Emma Elizabeth Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1572년 10월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 웨스트미에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 호k후르스트에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Emma Elizabeth Grey em 1520, em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de outubro de 1572, em Westmill, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Hawkhurst, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside родился 26 июля 1500 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его мать звали Margaret Girling. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma Elizabeth Grey в 1520 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 12 октября 1572 г. в местности вестмилл, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности ховхурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
